source_commit: 07b3635 task6-ddr3: prepare v97 master-event WDF pop
interpretation: v97 changed the native failure mode from all-zero readback to stable nonzero readback. Popping on the master/PHY write-data event keeps the FIFO nonempty at the captured event, and native writes now affect memory. The remaining bug is data correctness/advancement: all sampled native reads return the same wrong 64-bit word instead of the per-address expected pattern. This points to wrong beat/phase/chunk selection, FIFO pointer/beat advancement, or DFI mask/data lane mapping rather than missing native writes entirely.
next_action: For v98, keep master-event pop and expose enough data-side evidence to determine whether the same FIFO word is being replayed or whether the wrong DFI beat/lane is selected. Candidate status: capture push_count/pop_count/master_event_count/slave_event_count in visible payload, and/or encode low bits of task6_native_wdf_data_out/we_out at the captured event.
